name: Sm Muffins or Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 1 cup nonfat dry milk plus 2 tablespoons nonfat dry milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the baking powder, dry milk and salt into the sifted flour.
2. Sift all dry ingredients together until well mixed.
3. Cut or mix the fat into the flour mixture until all particles are thoroughly coated and the mixture resembles coarse cornmeal.
4. Store the mixture in a jar or can with a tight lid. It can be measured into 2 cup amounts and put in plastic bags or jars where it will be ready for use. It may be stored at room temperature for 6 weeks.
5. Vary the liquids: dry milk solids are already in the mix so more milk is not needed. You can use fruit juice, water, or meat broth for the liquid called for in a recipe.
The recipes in this Pick-A-pocket book were written for commercial packaged baking mix which has no milk, so it is the liquid called for in recipes. This mix is a perfect substitute. Just use water where it calls for milk.
Basic biscuits: 2 cups mix, 1/2 cup water. Add water to mix and stir 20-25 times. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead 10-15 times. Roll to 1/2 inch thickness and cut with a knife into pocket shapes or use a biscuit cutter to make circles. Place in preheated oven and bake for 5 minutes or until golden brown.
Sausage biscuits: 2 1/4 cups packaged biscuit baking mix (milk), 8 links sausages cooked. Prepare biscuits according to package directions for rolled biscuits. Knead dough, roll out on a floured surface and cut into 8 circles with a biscuit cutter. Lay one sausage in the center of each and wrap dough around, pinching to seal. Sausages will stick out the ends. Bake for 5 minutes or until golden brown.
Jelly biscuits: Flatten unbaked biscuits with the heel of your hand. Place 1/2 teaspoon jelly or preserves in the center. Fold over and pinch edges together to seal. Bake for 5 minutes or until golden.
Pimento cheese bread: 2 cups packaged biscuit baking mix, 1 egg beaten; 1/2 cup grated cheddar; 2/3 cup milk; 2 tablespoons drained diced pimento (optional). Mix all ingredients together until soft dough forms. Spoon into pockets. Bake for 10 minutes or until golden.
